mission.title: cilium: CFP: Add support for k8s Gateway API
mission.description: CFP: Add support for k8s Gateway API. This issue affects 61+ users.

steps.title: Identify cilium feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your cilium deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=cilium
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: CFP: Add support for k8s Gateway API

steps.title: Check current Service configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cilium resources:
```bash
kubectl get service -A
kubectl describe service <name> -n <namespace>
```
## Cilium Feature Proposal

This issue is to discuss and track for [k8s API Gateway][1] work mentioned in [roadmap][2]. Kindly note that the actual work or implementation might vary based on the discu

steps.title: Apply the fix for CFP: Add support for k8s Gateway API
steps.description:
This PR changes the naming of Envoy Listeners and Routes resources.
To avoid possible name clashing between resources in different namespaces, each Envoy Listener and Route name is prepended with its CEC namespace and name.
This is done while translating the CEC specifications to generate the Envoy config, and not during CEC creation. Thanks to this, a user generated CEC does not need to use the format <cecNamespace-cecName-resourceName> to be similar to an operator-generated CEC.

Relates #1969

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm CFP: Add support for k8s Gateway API is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: This PR changes the naming of Envoy Listeners and Routes resources.
To avoid possible name clashing between resources in different namespaces, each Envoy Listener and Route name is prepended with its CEC namespace and name.
This is done while translating the CEC specifications to generate the Envoy config, and not during CEC creation. Thanks to this, a user generated CEC does not need to use the f. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cilium installed or the issue environment reproducible.
